primary_bug.summary: The optimistic canonical-cast semantics are wired into the SHARED CanCastTo used by consumers beyond the intended dataflow/constraint-validation path, and they are unsound for definite cast decisions. CastingHelper.cs's CanCastToInternal now early-returns TRUE when the target is a canonical type (IsCanonicalCastTarget: __Canon accepts any GC pointer, __UniversalCanon accepts anything), and CanCastToClass/CanCastToNonVariantInterface/variance checks accept canon-equivalent matches (IsCanonEquivalent/IsCanonicalTypeArgMatch). These 'would be castable for SOME runtime instantiation' wildcard answers are correct for the PR's goal (constraint validation during ILC dataflow analysis) but WRONG wherever CanCastTo's answer is used as a definite 'this cast always succeeds/fails' — and the Canon flavor is compiled into ILCompiler.TypeSystem, which crossgen2 (ReadyToRun) also consumes for its compile-time cast optimization queries on shared-generics (canonical) types. Result: R2R-CG2 compiled casts incorrectly (folded/elided based on unsound answers), producing wrong runtime results — the JIT/opt/Casts/shared_Casts test regressed (Expected: 100, Actual: 0) on every R2R-CG2 platform, and the PR was reverted wholesale.
primary_bug.locus: IsCanonicalCastTarget / IsCanonEquivalent / IsCanonicalTypeArgMatch (CastingHelper.Canon.cs) + their call sites added in Common/CastingHelper.cs (CanCastToInternal early return ~L173, CanCastToClass, CanCastToNonVariantInterface, CanCastByVarianceToInterfaceOrDelegate) + the project wiring in ILCompiler.TypeSystem.csproj that gives this behavior to every ILCompiler.TypeSystem consumer (including crossgen2).
primary_bug.must_flag: A reviewer catches the primary bug if it flags ANY of: (a) the canonical-cast semantics are injected into the SHARED CanCastTo, changing behavior for OTHER consumers of the common type system (crossgen2/ReadyToRun, or 'other CanCastTo callers' generally) that were not the PR's target — i.e., the change is not scoped to the constraint-validation/dataflow path; (b) the new wildcard answers ('__Canon accepts any reference type') are optimistic MAY-cast semantics, unsound where CanCastTo is used for definite compile-time cast decisions/optimization (a canonical type stands for an UNKNOWN concrete type, so 'castable for some instantiation' must not be treated as 'castable for all'); (c) concretely, that R2R/crossgen2 cast optimization or non-dataflow CanCastTo callers get an untested behavior change (the added tests cover only constraint validation, not casting consumers). Vague 'this is complex/risky' or style notes about the partial-file pattern do NOT count; the catch must connect the shared/optimistic semantics to a wrong-answer risk for a definite-decision consumer.
primary_bug.source: Reverted by #127301 ('Revert Handle canonical types in casting logic', resolves #127259). Regression issue #127259: JIT/opt/Casts/shared_Casts fails (Assert.Equal Expected: 100, Actual: 0) on ALL R2R-CG2 configurations (windows/linux/osx, x86/x64/arm64) — crossgen2-compiled shared-generics casts produced wrong results. Foreshadowed in review by jkotas questioning the over-broad Canon-flavor inclusion (thread on System.Private.TypeLoader.csproj), with the author replying the rule was 'if you include .Canon.cs file, you include .Canon.cs files' and 'we can fix the bug if this ever becomes a bug.'
primary_bug.difficulty: very hard — deep JIT/AOT canonical-generics domain. Requires knowing that ILCompiler.TypeSystem is shared with crossgen2 and that CanCastTo feeds definite compile-time cast folding there. Authored by a principal NativeAOT engineer, reviewed by the .NET type-system architect (jkotas), and it still escaped, discovered only by outerloop R2R test runs. Expect very low recall; even a partial catch (flagging the shared-consumer blast radius or the may-vs-must semantics conflation) is a strong signal.

human_issues.summary: Over-broad inclusion of the real Canon casting behavior: System.Private.TypeLoader.csproj (the runtime type loader) is given CastingHelper.Canon.cs although it likely does not need canonical cast semantics — the author's rule 'if you include .Canon.cs, you include all .Canon.cs files' widens the behavior change beyond where it is required, acknowledged with 'we can fix the bug if this ever becomes a bug.' A reviewer should question which consumers actually need the Canon flavor.
human_issues.thread: jkotas: 'Do we actually need the real CastingHelper.Canon.cs in the runtime type loader?' — MichalStrehovsky: 'Probably not right now... It felt like an easier rule... I can make it include .NonCanon.cs here and we can fix the bug if this ever becomes a bug.' — jkotas: 'ok' (resolved, merged as-is)

known_safe.summary: The smoke test's body-less type declarations (`struct Atom;`, `class Gen<T, U, V> where U : IFoo, new();`, `interface IFoo;`, `class Foo : IFoo;` in Dataflow.cs) ARE valid modern C# (semicolon class bodies) and compile fine — the Copilot bot's claim that they 'will fail to compile' was wrong and was dismissed. A tool flagging these as syntax errors / non-compiling is a FALSE POSITIVE.

The NonCanon partial files (CastingHelper.NonCanon.cs, TypeSystemConstraintsHelpers.NonCanon.cs) with methods stubbed to `false` are the intentional no-op flavor of the partial-class pattern — projects that don't model canonical types include them so the shared code compiles with canonical handling disabled. Flagging them as dead/pointless code is a false positive; questioning WHICH projects get which flavor is the primary bug's territory.

The added `instantiatedType.NormalizeInstantiation()` in HandleCallAction.MakeGenericTypeSite is a deliberate fix for denormalized shapes (Foo<object, __Canon>) produced by InstantiateSignature, with an explanatory comment. Flagging the normalization itself as a bug is a false positive.

clusters.summary: The canon-widened shared CanCastTo violates the JIT-EE compareTypesForCast contract: IsCanonicalCastTarget/IsCanonEquivalent make e.g. IFoo<__Canon>.CanCastTo(IFoo<string>) return TRUE ('castable for SOME instantiation'), but CorInfoImpl.compareTypesForCast passes positive results through as TypeCompareState.Must — its own comment table documents this case as May. The JIT/crossgen2 then folds/elides required runtime casts for shared generics, a type-safety miscompile (both ILC and R2R; the READYTORUN sanitizer only converts MustNot, not the new wrong Must).
clusters.rationale: The precise primary mechanism: optimistic canon-widened CanCastTo answers flow through CorInfoImpl.compareTypesForCast as TypeCompareState.Must (its own table says May), so JIT/crossgen2 folds required runtime casts — the exact type-safety miscompile that produced #127259 and the revert.

clusters.summary: System.Private.TypeLoader.csproj is given the REAL CastingHelper.Canon.cs (not the behavior-preserving NonCanon stub), silently changing the runtime type loader's CanCastTo semantics used by variant GVM dispatch resolution — a runtime behavior change with no test and no PR-visible justification (the 'include all .Canon.cs files' rule).
clusters.rationale: Exactly the jkotas thread: System.Private.TypeLoader.csproj gets the REAL CastingHelper.Canon.cs (diff lines 9-11), silently changing runtime type-loader CanCastTo semantics with no test and only the 'include all .Canon.cs files' rule as justification.

clusters.summary: Every other consumer of the widened CanCastTo inherits the new optimistic semantics unaudited: DevirtualizationManager's guards, MetadataVirtualMethodAlgorithm's DIM/static-virtual variant resolution, MethodDesc covariant-return compatibility, TypeExtensions.MergeTypesToCommonParent/TryResolveConstraintMethodApprox, crossgen2's TypeValidationChecker/UnsafeAccessors, and ~10 downstream projects referencing ILCompiler.TypeSystem — none adjusted or tested by the PR.
clusters.rationale: Matches must_flag (a)/(c): names OTHER consumers of the widened shared CanCastTo (devirtualization guards, DIM/static-virtual resolution, covariant returns, crossgen2 TypeValidationChecker, downstream projects) inheriting the optimistic semantics unaudited and untested — the blast-radius that caused the revert.

clusters.summary: MakeGenericMethodSite.InstantiateDependencies did NOT receive the NormalizeInstantiation() fix its structural twin MakeGenericTypeSite got, so MakeGenericMethod dataflow still checks constraints on (and roots) denormalized shapes — the same hazard class (#126604) on the method path, untested. (Some reviewers argue it is defensible via downstream GetCanonMethodTarget canonicalization.)
clusters.rationale: Verified in diff: only MakeGenericTypeSite receives NormalizeInstantiation(); the parallel MakeGenericMethod dataflow path is left checking constraints on denormalized shapes — plausible incomplete-fix asymmetry, untested, though downstream canonicalization may mitigate.

clusters.summary: The new canonical branches of the real CanCastTo surface are essentially untested: IsCanonicalCastTarget's true-returning arms never execute in any test (deleting the hook leaves the suite green), no test exercises the broadened CanCastTo outside constraint checking, and there is zero coverage of compareTypesForCast/devirtualization/variance consumers with canonical inputs.
clusters.rationale: Matches must_flag (c): the broadened CanCastTo has zero coverage outside constraint checking and none for compareTypesForCast/devirtualization consumers with canonical inputs; traced the tests — CanCastToConstraintWithCanon intercepts canon-target cases before IsCanonicalCastTarget, so its true-arms plausibly never execute.

clusters.summary: Doc/code mismatches in the new helpers: the 'pointers/byrefs/function pointers are not valid instantiation arguments' comment corresponds to no code check (the __UniversalCanon branch returns true unconditionally for them), IsCanonicalCastTarget's deliberate one-directional asymmetry is undocumented, and IsCanonicalTypeArgMatch's summary omits the structural/recursive half.
clusters.rationale: Verified doc mismatches: the pointers/byrefs/fnptr sentence in IsCanonicalCastTarget has no corresponding code check (Universal branch returns true unconditionally) and IsCanonicalTypeArgMatch's summary omits its structural/recursive half — doc-level.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Bundle of doc quibbles: the pointers/byrefs comment states an external invariant rather than contradicting code, the asymmetry point is could-document-more, and the summary omission ('because one is canonical' loosely covers the recursive case) does not actively mislead.

clusters.summary: The __UniversalCanon<->__Canon cross-form matching (the '|| IsCanonicalDefinitionType(..., Any)' disjuncts) is unexplained and arguably over-permissive — __UniversalCanon also stands for value types that __Canon cannot represent.
clusters.rationale: The UC<->Canon cross-form disjuncts are consistent with the PR's intended optimistic 'castable for SOME instantiation' semantics (UC may stand for a ref type); 'unexplained' is a documentation note, not a distinct defect.
clusters.regrade_rationale: 'Arguably over-permissive' + 'unexplained' — speculative correctness concern about canon semantics with no concrete pathway, plus a could-document-more ask.

clusters.summary: Function-pointer canon shapes (e.g. delegate*<string,void> vs delegate*<__Canon,void>) hit no case in IsCanonicalTypeArgMatch and silently return false, reproducing #126604's bug class for function-pointer generics.
clusters.rationale: The diff's own doc comment declares pointers/byrefs/function pointers invalid as instantiation arguments, so the direct delegate*<__Canon,...> arg scenario cannot arise; any residual nested case fails conservatively (returns false = pre-PR behavior), not unsoundly.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Refuted by the diff's own documented contract ('function pointers are not valid instantiation arguments', CastingHelper.Canon.cs comment) — speculative missed-case with no concrete pathway, and the helper is an additive short-circuit (false = pre-PR behavior).

clusters.summary: The Canon/NonCanon partial-file selection contract is entirely undocumented and only half-enforced: compile errors catch a missing/double include, but a WRONG choice is silent (a project picking NonCanon silently resurrects #126604; picking Canon silently widens semantics). C#9 extended partial methods (permitted at this LangVersion) would declare the seam; the stubs carry zero explanatory comments.
clusters.rationale: Real design gap: flavor selection is undocumented and silent-on-wrong-choice (compile errors only catch missing/double includes) — the mechanism that actually bit — but stated abstractly, naming neither definite-decision consumers (not primary) nor a specific over-broad project (not h1).

clusters.summary: The canon-wildcard kernel rule is re-encoded in three/four places (CanCastToConstraintWithCanon duplicates IsCanonicalCastTarget verbatim; IsSpecialTypeMeetingConstraint re-derives it), inviting silent drift between copies.
clusters.rationale: Verified: the Universal->true / Specific->IsGCPointer kernel is re-encoded in IsCanonicalCastTarget, CanCastToConstraintWithCanon, and IsCanonicalTypeArgMatch — real DRY/drift concern, but structural/maintainability, not a defect.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Duplication is real but the fix crosses partial-class boundaries (CastingHelper vs TypeSystemConstraintsHelpers private helpers) — not one-shot, design-taste dedup maintainers would debate; 'inviting silent drift' is speculative.

clusters.summary: IsCanonEquivalent's doc ('same type definition with all type arguments equal or canon-compatible') contradicts the code: the Length==0 early-return makes identical NON-generic pairs return false, an unexplained asymmetry.
clusters.rationale: The Length==0 early-return vs doc asymmetry is real, but every call site ORs IsCanonEquivalent with IsEquivalentTo (or checks == first), so identical non-generic pairs are already handled — doc-precision only.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Objective doc/code mismatch: summary says 'same type definition with all type arguments either equal or canon-compatible' but the Length==0 early-return makes identical non-generic pairs return false — one-shot doc correction.

clusters.summary: IsCanonEquivalent's equal-arity invariant is implicit (no Debug.Assert unlike the sibling with identical logic), and the per-arg loop uses reference equality (==) instead of IsEquivalentTo.
clusters.rationale: Equal arity is guaranteed by HasSameTypeDefinition and == on interned TypeDescs is the codebase idiom; missing Debug.Assert is a defensive-style note.
clusters.regrade_rationale: HasSameTypeDefinition already implies equal arity (no reachable failure), and == on interned TypeDesc is the codebase norm — both halves are debatable defensive/taste suggestions, not one-shot accepted fixes.

clusters.summary: Multiple new-code branches are untested: array rank/category mismatches (only rank-1 SzArrays covered), canon-on-the-left branches (C/D) of IsCanonicalTypeArgMatch, the mixed equal+canon instantiation path of IsCanonEquivalent (the actual #126604 shape), and no negative array test (int[] vs __Canon[]).
clusters.rationale: Verified against the test diff: no multi-rank array test, no canon-on-the-left case, no mixed equal+canon multi-arg instantiation, and no negative array test (int[] vs __Canon[]) — real dark branches in the new code.

clusters.summary: The IsCanonicalTypeArgMatch/IsCanonEquivalent mutual recursion carries no StackOverflowProtect/cycle guard, unlike every other recursive walk in the file (validators note the recursion is structurally decreasing, so this is a consistency/defensive concern, not a live bug).
clusters.rationale: Cluster itself concedes the mutual recursion is structurally decreasing (finite type trees), so no live bug — consistency/defensive note about the missing StackOverflowProtect.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Finding's own text concedes the recursion is structurally decreasing (no live bug) — speculative defensive-consistency concern with no concrete pathway.

clusters.summary: The new helpers use TypeDesc.IsGCPointer, which is false for GenericParameterDesc even under a 'where T : class' constraint, unlike sibling code using HasReferenceTypeConstraint — an under-match concern (validators largely refuted a live impact since the checks are additive short-circuits).
clusters.rationale: Cluster self-refutes live impact: the new checks are additive short-circuits, so IsGCPointer being false for a constrained GenericParameterDesc just falls through to pre-existing logic (pre-PR behavior) — consistency note.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Finding's own text notes validators refuted live impact (additive short-circuits, false = pre-PR behavior) — speculative under-match with no concrete pathway.

clusters.summary: IsSpecialTypeMeetingConstraint has no doc comment despite encoding the least-obvious policy of the five new helpers (why __Canon meets class/new() but not struct).
clusters.rationale: True — IsSpecialTypeMeetingConstraint has no doc comment in the diff — but a missing doc comment is trivial.
clusters.regrade_rationale: All four sibling helpers introduced by this diff carry /// summaries; this one alone lacks one while encoding the subtlest policy — the diff's own demonstrated convention, one-shot doc fix that converges.

clusters.summary: IsSpecialTypeMeetingConstraint's NotNullableValueTypeConstraint arm appears to be dead code — the caller's guard is already false for __UniversalCanon (the only type that could match), so the arm can never fire.
clusters.rationale: If __UniversalCanon.IsValueType (which is how the UC struct-constraint test passes through the caller guard), the (!IsValueType || IsNullable) gate is false for UC and the NotNullableValueTypeConstraint arm can never fire — dead arm in new code, minor impact.

clusters.summary: Naming drift in the new code: parameter 'type' vs sibling convention 'thisType'; 'IsCanonEquivalent' short-form vs sibling 'Canonical'; IsSpecialTypeMeetingConstraint breaks the file's Check* convention; NonVariantGenImpl breaks the ArgN fixture convention.
clusters.rationale: Naming-drift observations (type vs thisType, IsCanonEquivalent short form, Check* convention) — pure style.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Naming grab-bag with shaky anchors: type/otherType is apt for a symmetric arg comparison, 'Canon' is established repo vocabulary (CanonType, .Canon.cs files), and no uniform Check* convention exists (CanCastConstraint, VerifyGenericParamConstraint).

clusters.summary: Unused `using System.Diagnostics;` left in TypeSystemConstraintsHelpers.NonCanon.cs — the Copilot bot flagged unused usings and the cleanup removed only one of the two (the Canon sibling genuinely needs it; the stub does not).
clusters.rationale: Verified: TypeSystemConstraintsHelpers.NonCanon.cs keeps 'using System.Diagnostics;' that its false-returning stubs never use — trivial cleanup miss.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Verified in the diff: NonCanon stub imports System.Diagnostics but uses nothing from it (Canon sibling needs it for UnreachableException) — objective, one-shot, converges, half-addressed bot feedback.

clusters.summary: Project-file style inconsistencies: CastingHelper.Canon.cs entries spliced into the alphabetized Common block instead of the dedicated Canon block (two csproj files), and the new <Link> element uses 8-space indentation vs the file's 6-space convention.
clusters.rationale: Verified in diff: CastingHelper.Canon.cs spliced into the Common block of ILCompiler.TypeSystem.csproj, and the new <Link> in ILVerification.projitems uses 8-space indent vs 6 — pure style.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Verified in the diff: ILVerification.projitems new <Link> uses 8 spaces vs the file's 6-space siblings, and CastingHelper.Canon.cs lands in the Common block while the same PR puts TypeSystemConstraintsHelpers.Canon.cs in the dedicated Canon block — the diff's own convention, one-shot.

clusters.summary: The pre-existing value-type prefilter fires before any canon-awareness, so e.g. 'where T : U' with T=MyStruct<string>, U=MyStruct<__Canon> returns false even though the canon logic would match — canonical value-type generic shapes are shadowed.
clusters.rationale: Verified against the constraint loop: composite canon value-type shapes (MyStruct<string> vs MyStruct<__Canon>) fall past CanCastToConstraintWithCanon (which only handles canon definition types) into the pre-existing value-type prefilter, which returns false before CanCastTo/IsCanonEquivalent could match — conservative under-match in the #126604 class.

clusters.summary: Undocumented ordering dependencies: CanCastToConstraintWithCanon must run before the value-type short-circuit (because __UniversalCanon.IsValueType), and IsCanonicalCastTarget's early return must precede the category switch — neither load-bearing placement is commented.
clusters.rationale: The orderings are genuinely load-bearing (CanCastToConstraintWithCanon before the value-type check saves UC from the IsValueType prefilter; IsCanonicalCastTarget before the category switch), but the ask is comments — doc-level.
clusters.regrade_rationale: 'Add comments explaining ordering' is a could-document-more class suggestion; the load-bearing claims are themselves inferential, not demonstrated from the diff.

clusters.summary: Comment-contract mismatches in the constraints helper: CanCastToConstraintWithCanon's doc reads as a caller precondition but it is invoked unconditionally, and the 'any concrete type substituted at runtime will be validated then' claim is an absolute runtime guarantee not verifiable from this diff.
clusters.rationale: Doc-phrasing issues only: the summary reads as a caller precondition though the method is invoked unconditionally (and correctly returns false otherwise); the runtime-validation claim is a comment assertion.
clusters.regrade_rationale: The 'when' clause is interpretive, not an objective contradiction (the method self-checks canonicity and is safe called unconditionally); the second half is meta commentary about what the diff can verify.

clusters.summary: Variance/base-chain integration points are only shallowly tested: no covariant (out T) fixture at all, CanCastToClass's base-walk canon match only exercised at zero-hop depth, the self-interface IsCanonEquivalent check never covered, and no negative variance-path test.
clusters.rationale: Verifiable against the test diff: no covariant (out T) fixture appears in the new tests, and the base-chain canon match (ComplexGenericConstraint2) is only hit at zero-hop — real shallow-coverage gaps on the casting integration points.

clusters.summary: Several new test assertions are vacuous/non-load-bearing: five Assert.True cases pass via pre-existing short-circuits (IsGCPointer etc.) before reaching the new canon code their comments credit, and two 'canonical variance' assertions pass via the ordinary variance path.
clusters.rationale: Spot-verified: __Canon is a GC pointer so the class-constraint Assert.True passes via the pre-existing IsGCPointer short-circuit, and the contravariant IGen cases pass via ordinary variance (__Canon.CanCastTo(object) is true pre-PR) — assertions vacuously credit the new code.

clusters.summary: Factually wrong/self-contradictory test comments: a block calls IGen<in T> contravariant then labels the same slot 'invariant arg position'; 'U=int[] stays' contradicts array canonicalization; and the 'Parameterized canonical types (__Canon[] ...)' block header promises a scenario its body never constructs (duplicate of an earlier case).
clusters.rationale: Verified in the test diff: the block calls IGen<in T> contravariant then says 'invariant arg position', and the 'Parameterized canonical types (__Canon[])' header's body never constructs __Canon[] (uses int[]+canon, duplicating an earlier case) — wrong comments, but comment-level.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Verified in the diff: the same comment block calls IGen<in T> contravariant then labels the slot 'invariant arg position', and the '__Canon[] parameterized canonical types' header fronts a body that never constructs __Canon[] — objective comment-vs-code correctness, one-shot fixes.

clusters.summary: The end-to-end smoke test is a weaker proxy for #126604: it exercises the wildcard-param branch rather than the structural interface match of the reported repro, has no negative case, and its only 'assertion' is .ToString() (did-not-throw).
clusters.rationale: True that the smoke test's only signal is did-not-throw (.ToString()) and it exercises the wildcard-param branch with no negative case; real test-quality gap, though did-not-throw is the standard pattern in this smoke-test file.

clusters.summary: The two new fixture types are fetched as method-locals inside the new [Fact], inconsistent with the class's 20+ constructor-initialized field fixtures.
clusters.rationale: Method-local vs field fixture placement is a pure style inconsistency.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Method-local fixtures scoped to the only test using them is a defensible structural choice, not an objective violation of the field-fixture convention — maintainers would debate the churn; borderline defaults down.

clusters.summary: Test-assembly fixture placement/naming quibbles: new types appended after the unrelated GenericMethods class rather than grouped with variant counterparts; NonVariantGenImpl diverges from the ArgN implementer naming.
clusters.rationale: Fixture placement/naming quibbles in the test assembly — pure style.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Self-described placement/naming quibbles — grouping and NonVariantGenImpl-vs-ArgN naming are taste-only with no objective in-repo anchor.

clusters.summary: RootingHelpers swallows all TypeSystemException from CheckCanGenerateMethod and returns false silently; when CheckConstraints fails the dependency list is silently empty with no diagnostic.
clusters.rationale: RootingHelpers.cs is pre-existing code outside the diff; an empty dependency list when CheckConstraints fails is the intended dataflow design (invalid shapes deliberately not rooted) — out-of-scope.
clusters.regrade_rationale: RootingHelpers.cs is not touched by this diff — pre-existing/out-of-scope error-swallowing behavior; the diff only added NormalizeInstantiation in HandleCallAction.

clusters.summary: No test exercises the canonical constraint logic with a non-null InstantiationContext, unlike the real production caller.
clusters.rationale: Verified: every new test calls CheckConstraints() with no InstantiationContext while the production caller in HandleCallAction.cs passes new InstantiationContext(typeInst, methodInst) — real, narrow coverage gap.

clusters.summary: Claim that System.Private.TypeLoader.csproj adds CastingHelper.Canon.cs but is missing the matching TypeSystemConstraintsHelpers Canon/NonCanon pair, unlike sibling projects. (Note: that project does not compile TypeSystemConstraintsHelpers.cs at all, so no pairing is required.)
clusters.rationale: Refuted: System.Private.TypeLoader does not compile TypeSystemConstraintsHelpers.cs at all (a missing partial flavor would be a compile error and the project builds), so no Canon/NonCanon pairing is required — the exact 'missing matching pair' FP named in the brief.

clusters.summary: The new smoke-test types use C# semicolon (empty-body) declarations, a style not used elsewhere in this particular file though established in sibling test files and fully valid at this LangVersion.
clusters.rationale: Explicitly concedes the semicolon body declarations are valid at this LangVersion (correctly avoids the safe-bodyless-types false positive); remaining claim is file-local style only.
clusters.regrade_rationale: Finding's own text concedes the semicolon-body style is established in sibling test files and fully valid — taste-only; a brace-body 'fix' would be rejected churn.
